evidence: ORIGINAL spec.tla:429-431: anchor == IF ob_backup_applied[m] /= {} THEN MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m]) ELSE 0. When ob_backup_applied[m]={}, anchor=0, so recovery calls MaxContiguousFrom(1, available), scanning from seq 1 (full log). MODIFIED spec_modified.tla:444-446: anchor == IF ob_backup_applied[m] /= {} THEN MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m]) ELSE ob_checkpoint_seq[m]. When ob_backup_applied[m]={}, anchor=ob_checkpoint_seq[m], recovery calls MaxContiguousFrom(ob_checkpoint_seq[m]+1, available), scanning only the post-checkpoint tail. OBCheckpoint (spec_modified.tla:423-429) sets ob_checkpoint_seq[m] = ob_seq[m] - 1 (the highest committed seq) whenever ob_alive[m] and ob_seq[m]>1, confirming the checkpoint records a meaningful watermark not just a default. The MinOfTwo cap is at spec_modified.tla:457: ob_checkpoint_seq' = [ob_checkpoint_seq EXCEPT ![m] = MinOfTwo(@, recovered)], which pulls the checkpoint back to the recovered watermark when archivers have less data than the stale checkpoint value, preventing cross-session staleness.
before_after_comparison: BEFORE (original, line 431): anchor=0 when backup empty. MaxContiguousFrom(1, available) must scan the entire archiver set starting from seq 1. Worst case: full log reconstruction from seq 1 to current ob_seq. AFTER (modified, line 446): anchor=ob_checkpoint_seq[m] when backup empty. If a checkpoint was taken at seq K (meaning ob_seq was K+1 at checkpoint time), then anchor=K and MaxContiguousFrom(K+1, available) scans only the post-checkpoint segment. Recovery scope shrinks from [1..current] to [K+1..current]. The MinOfTwo cap (line 457) handles the double-crash case: if session N sets ob_checkpoint_seq=9 but session N+1 only recovers to seq 7 (archivers incomplete), the cap sets ob_checkpoint_seq=min(9,7)=7, so session N+2 starts with anchor=7, not the stale 9, preventing a gap at seq 9 that would stall OMS.
caveats: Recovery scope improvement is only realized when OBCheckpoint fires before the crash. If ob_seq[m] never exceeds 1 (OBCheckpoint guard: ob_seq[m]>1 at line 425) or OBCheckpoint simply never fires, ob_checkpoint_seq stays at 0 (init, line 175) and behavior is identical to BEFORE. The proposal improves worst-case recovery scope but the improvement is contingent on checkpoint liveness.

OBCheckpoint uses ob_seq[m]-1 as the checkpoint value (line 426), which is the last seq written, not the last seq flushed to archivers. If the archiver write for seq ob_seq[m]-1 has not completed before a crash, the checkpoint points to a seq that is not yet in available, causing MaxContiguousFrom to return a lower value. The MinOfTwo cap then corrects this, but the anchor can temporarily exceed the archiver state. This is safe (MinOfTwo ensures recovered<=checkpoint) but means the effective scope reduction may be smaller than the checkpoint gap suggests.
